evals.prompt: What have you learned about me from my job search so far? (No `.job-hunter/` directory exists in the workspace yet — the user has not run the job-hunter orchestrator or applied to anything.)
evals.expected_output: Skill detects there is no `.job-hunter/` workspace to read, reports honestly that there is nothing to learn from yet, and points the user at the orchestrator (or job-search) to set up the workspace and start applying. Does NOT fabricate lessons or outcomes. Does NOT claim to own or create the workspace — the `.job-hunter/` directory and its templates are owned by the orchestrator's init_workspace.py.

assertions.text: Skill reports there is no learning-loop data yet because the .job-hunter/ workspace does not exist
Skill does NOT fabricate any LESSONS, OUTCOMES, or patterns from a non-existent workspace (cold-start respect)
Skill points the user at the job-hunter orchestrator (or job-search) to initialize the workspace and start applying, rather than initializing it itself
Skill does NOT claim to own or create the .job-hunter/ templates (those are the orchestrator's init_workspace.py per the workspace-contract)

evals.prompt: What have you learned about me from my job search? (.job-hunter/REJECTED_IDEAS.md contains a single entry: '2026-04-15, User: no government contractor or defense work. Reason: cleared work blocked their last career change.' OUTCOMES.md has 6 closed entries.)
evals.expected_output: Skill reads REJECTED_IDEAS.md and treats 'no government contractor or defense work' as a hard constraint that is already on file — it reports it as a standing filter, does NOT re-ask the user about defense/government preferences, and does NOT propose adding or re-confirming it as a lesson. It only ever READS REJECTED_IDEAS.md; it never auto-writes to it.

assertions.text: Skill reads .job-hunter/REJECTED_IDEAS.md and surfaces the standing hard constraint
Skill does NOT re-ask the user about defense/government preferences (already on file)
Skill does NOT propose adding entries to REJECTED_IDEAS.md — only the user adds rejected ideas (never auto-write)
Skill distinguishes the hard constraint (REJECTED_IDEAS) from confirmed preferences (LESSONS) in its reasoning

evals.prompt: Summarize what you'd tell job-search to do differently for me. (.job-hunter/LESSONS.md contains the entry: '2026-04-20, User consistently rejects companies over 5000 employees. Confirmed across 7 of 9 closed-loop rejections in OUTCOMES.md citing too bureaucratic or too political.')
evals.expected_output: Skill reads LESSONS.md and reports that confirmed lessons shape how the family GRADES sub-scores for this user (e.g., grade cultural_signals lower for large companies), citing the lesson so the user can audit. It is explicit that lessons influence grading, NOT the scoring weights in score_posting.py (owned by job-search), which stay constant for all users. It applies the lesson only to the relevant sub-score (cultural_signals), not to unrelated ones (bounded influence).

assertions.text: Skill reads .job-hunter/LESSONS.md and reports the confirmed pattern
Skill explains the lesson adjusts how cultural_signals is GRADED for this user, citing the lesson for auditability
Skill is explicit that lessons do NOT change the scoring weights in score_posting.py (owned by job-search; weights stay constant)
Skill applies the lesson only to the relevant sub-score (cultural_signals), not to unrelated sub-scores (bounded influence)

evals.prompt: What have you learned about me? (.job-hunter/OUTCOMES.md contains only 3 closed-loop entries.)
evals.expected_output: Skill runs harvest_outcomes.py + propose_lessons.py, gets back no_op_reason because there are fewer than 5 closed-loop outcomes (cold-start guard), and tells the user honestly: 'I do not have enough closed-loop outcomes yet to suggest patterns — I need at least 5, you have 3.' Does NOT fabricate patterns from thin data.

assertions.text: Skill runs harvest_outcomes.py (does not skip the harvest)
Skill reports the cold-start guard honestly (need >=5, have 3)
Skill does NOT fabricate or paraphrase lessons from <5 outcomes
Skill suggests how to get more signal (apply to more roles + update tracker.json status) rather than declaring done

evals.prompt: What have you learned about me? (.job-hunter/OUTCOMES.md contains 6 closed-loop entries: 4 of 6 marked 'rejected, comp below target' and 2 of 6 marked 'accepted offer'.)
evals.expected_output: Skill runs harvest_outcomes.py + propose_lessons.py, receives a suggested LESSON ('comp_vs_target is the dominant rejection reason in 4 of 6 outcomes, consider grading comp more strictly'), surfaces the suggestion to the user with the underlying evidence, and asks for confirmation BEFORE appending to LESSONS.md. Does NOT auto-append.

assertions.text: Skill runs both harvest_outcomes.py and propose_lessons.py
Skill surfaces the proposed LESSON with the underlying evidence (4 of 6 rejections, the specific reason)
Skill asks the user 'Want me to remember this?' before appending to .job-hunter/LESSONS.md
Skill does NOT auto-append the suggestion to LESSONS.md without user confirmation
If user confirms, skill appends a properly formatted entry to LESSONS.md (the script-formatted lesson_block) with date + reason + evidence count
